features.description: Desktop apps are the planned local MCP bridge; mobile apps remain local script runners without a listening MCP endpoint.

macos.reason: macOS should host an in-app local MCP server backed by the existing script engine and transports.

evidence.contains: macOSKEEPremovedlive folderin-process

ios.reason: iOS keeps user script import/app-local execution and does not host an external MCP listener.

evidence.contains: iOS / iPadOSKEEPremovedimport into app

windows.reason: Windows should host an in-app local MCP server backed by the existing script engine and transports.

evidence.contains: WindowsKEEPremovedlive folderin-process

android.reason: Android keeps user script import/app-local execution and does not host an external MCP listener.

evidence.contains: AndroidKEEPremovedimport into app

features.description: The desktop MCP contract exposes script lifecycle, device state, and hardware primitive tools.

macos.reason: macOS has the desktop runtime and should host the first in-app MCP bridge.

ios.reason: Mobile does not expose an external MCP server.

windows.reason: Windows tracks the desktop runtime direction and should expose the same MCP tool contract.

android.reason: Mobile does not expose an external MCP server.

features.description: In-app Agent chat, MGPT API keys, universe persistence, and MGPT responses clients are being removed from all native apps.

evidence.contains: Rip out the Agent — all five apps
Rip out MGPT — all five apps
